name: shadcn/ui blocks guide
description: Use composable blocks (auth, dashboards, tables, settings) to ship faster; keep ownership and avoid rigid libraries.

metadata.intent: Adopt shadcn UI blocks to speed delivery without sacrificing a11y or ownership

must_not: Adopt block libraries as opaque runtime dependencies if they fight ownership
conventions: Copy code, own it, adapt to product logic; keep states and a11y correct.

steps.description: Blocks = pre-assembled sections built from multiple shadcn components (dashboard shells, auth flows, tables, settings pages). You copy JSX and adapt it—no rigid props API lock-in.

Common block categories: dashboards (shell/sidebar/topbar/widgets), auth (login/register/verify/reset/2FA), data (tables/charts/stats cards), forms & settings, marketing (hero/features/pricing/testimonials/faq/cta/footer).
Ownership rule: blocks should live in `components/blocks/` and be product-aware. Avoid copying the same block into multiple pages—centralize and parameterize with minimal props.
When you adapt blocks, preserve: loading/empty/error states, keyboard navigation, focus order, labels, and responsive layout. Run `pi validate` and fix the deterministic a11y findings first.
